- Benefits - Time off policy - Nice office with healthy snacks and customizable cubicles - Roomy meeting spaces - Mostly 40-hour work week - Company-issued laptop and optional phone plan - 3 days per week in office/remote option - I was allowed to do the Hunger Games three-finger salute on my last day lol Great Company, Bad Clients

evidence.role: Former employee - Senior Scrum Master/Agile Coach
evidence.text: Way too much politics involved on vertical !!! Unfortunately, the line managers and next hierarchical boss have been trained at Stevie Wonder`s school of business - they have NO vision and NO direction, but gossip and pointing fingers is in their manuals. When the project was completed because the client wanted something different or looking for someone else, Capgemini did nothing to find a new client! Bench is the...

evidence.role: Former employee - Software Development Engineer In Test (SDET)
evidence.text: If your position ends you are not going to be able to get additional customer easily Great Support
Not friendly and hard to engage Overall fine
Salary is less and no bonus Working as a software engineer at Capgemini

a lot of politics, nepotism, favoritism, sexism, pretty much a lot of isms but considering most places I've worked in have been pretty bad, this is the best of the worst. Good company to work in, and stay in

1. If you're not in a "leading city" "big city" your job is not secure 2. Constant stress and anxiety about being laid offs, that caused me health problems 3. No job security, no matter the number of certifications you receive you're not safe if you're not billable 4. Promotions are hard to come around One Year Review

Only problem is that you will be subjugated to market conditions and how stable your client is. If you are put on a project with an instable client you might find yourself and the team you worked with on the bench before you know what happened. It's a high stress environment and your client might have a toxic work environment that you will need to live with. Chaos
$77 Limited Role Transition Opportunities - Lots of politics, but good brand equity for a stepping stone
No job security Worst Management Poor Communication No Job safety
